human_use.rationale: The Artifact is a human-operated mobile checkout. Its success depends on discoverability, navigation, input burden, error recovery, feedback, accessibility, touch ergonomics, interruption recovery, perceived latency, destructive-action safety, and cognitive load across cross-disability use.

track.label: Single-page landmarked checkout
track.hypothesis: A single landmarked checkout with a persistent order summary minimizes context switching while headings, fieldsets, error summary, and skip or landmark navigation preserve nonvisual orientation.
track.allowed_change_scope: .experiments/accessible-mobile-checkout/generated/track-single-page/** only; the shared harness and other Tracks are read-only.
track.interaction_paradigm: All required checkout sections remain present and landmarked in one form, with a sticky order summary at mobile widths.

iterations.hypothesis: A single landmarked checkout with a persistent order summary minimizes context switching while headings, fieldsets, error summary, and skip or landmark navigation preserve nonvisual orientation.
iterations.outcome: The final frozen objective run exited 0 with all seven gates passing, no failed gate IDs, and no blocking failure. Independent qualitative review remains pending.

prompt.track_prompt: See track-single-page/loop-01/prompt-history.md. Execute exactly one generator-single-page Loop in the single-page Track, preserve all frozen invariants and the landmarked single-page paradigm, run the unchanged objective harness, and leave independent qualitative review pending.
prompt.judge_feedback: None; no independent judge was run.
prompt.next_prompt: After independent identity-blind findings are available, authorize a separate Loop 02 only to apply one evidence-backed improvement while preserving this Track paradigm and every frozen invariant.

lesson.trigger: Early objective attempts exposed candidate-to-contract incompatibilities in fill behavior, shipping hook text, and final-action focus measurement.
lesson.action: Expose fillable state input, synchronized shipping option text and checked state at each required hook, and a pinned high-contrast final-action outline.
lesson.evidence: The same frozen harness moved from five failed gates, to two failed gates, to all seven gates passing.

iterations.stop_reason: Exactly one candidate Loop was requested; objective gates pass and independent qualitative judging intentionally remains pending.

iterations.hypothesis: Optional compact completed-section summaries and grouped error navigation reduce traversal and error-state load while native autofill, native radios, and shape-valid synthetic validation reduce interaction burden without leaving the single-page landmarked paradigm.
iterations.outcome: Preserved attempt 02 passed all seven frozen objective gates. Requested-change checks pass, but qualitative comparison is pending independent judges and no new_best claim is made.

prompt.track_prompt: See track-single-page/loop-02/prompt-history.md. Execute exactly one generator-single-page improvement Loop from immutable Loop 01, consume the bound exact feedback, preserve the single-page landmarked form and every frozen invariant, and run the unchanged harness.
prompt.input_feedback:
Within the single-page landmarked paradigm, reduce traversal and error-state load with compact completed-section summaries or optional progressive disclosure that keeps landmarks and direct editability; restore appropriate autocomplete tokens, use native radio inputs styled as cards without duplicate ARIA roles, and relax exact-fixture validation to shape-valid synthetic inputs while retaining clear fixture hints and all frozen invariants.

Source: judging/loop-01/judge-accessibility/candidate-b.json
Attribution: judge-accessibility defect
At 390px, the complete one-page form and 13-item error state are cognitively and physically long; the user must manage many fields before review.

Source: judging/loop-01/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json
Attribution: judge-human-use input burden finding
Validation is strict-fixture: state must equal 'NSW' (case-sensitive), country must equal 'australia' (lowercased); card number must equal '4111111111111111'; expiry must equal '12/34'; CSC must equal '123'; cardholder must equal 'alex morgan'. Any real-world variation is rejected. All inputs also have autocomplete='off', which disables browser autofill / password-manager assistance — a documented accessibility anti-pattern for motor-constrained and cognitively-impaired users. The 'Synthetic value:' helper text and fixture-card do mitigate for sighted users who read them, but the underlying interaction burden is high. Evidence: candidate-b/app.js fieldRules + errorMessages, candidate-b/index.html autocomplete='off' on every input.

Source: judging/loop-01/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json
Attribution: judge-human-use accessibility finding
Solid landmark tree (header/nav/main/aside/footer), fieldset+legend per section, aria-describedby chained, error summary role='alert' with tabindex=-1 that receives focus after invalid submit. Concerns: (a) Shipping uses a double-radio anti-pattern — <label class='choice-card' role='radio' aria-checked> wrapping a real <input type='radio' aria-hidden='true'> — screen-reader users may hear the group twice or miss state updates because aria-checked is set only via the keydown handler, not by the input's own change; (b) autocomplete='off' on every field is an anti-pattern for AT-assisted form filling; (c) no visible skip-link even though the section-nav helps sighted keyboard users. Evidence: candidate-b/index.html lines 171–184; candidate-b/app.js keydown handler on shipping-* elements.

Source: judging/loop-01/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json
Attribution: judge-human-use cognitive load finding
All five sections are on one continuously scrolled page. Nav pills help orientation, but ~20 required fields plus fixture cards plus a review block are visible at once, which is heavy on a small viewport. The exact-match validation adds hidden rules ('why won't it accept 4111-1111-1111-1111 with dashes?') that a real cross-disability user must reason through. The review block is helpful but reprints only aggregate values rather than every field verbatim, so users must scroll back to correct.
prompt.judge_feedback: Bound independent Loop 01 feedback only; no Loop 02 judge was run.
prompt.next_prompt: Have approved independent judges operate Loop 02 against every frozen qualitative lens and compare it with Loop 01, preserving dissent. Keep needs_human_review until that evidence exists.

lesson.trigger: Independent feedback tied long traversal, a 13-item error panorama, blanket autocomplete suppression, duplicate radio semantics, and exact-fixture rejection to avoidable cross-disability burden.
lesson.action: Keep all landmarks and direct editing, but make completed-section compaction optional, group the summary by affected section with full details on demand, use native controls/autocomplete, and validate synthetic input shape rather than exact identity.
lesson.evidence: The frozen harness passes all seven gates; supplemental local checks pass all five requested-change assertions, including a successful noncanonical shape-valid synthetic completion and native arrow-key radio update.

iterations.stop_reason: Exactly one authorized improvement Loop completed. All objective gates pass, but Loop 02 qualitative judging was not authorized and remains pending, so no new_best promotion is claimed.
